compartments.label: NEURONS
compartments.snippet: Large central compartment in panel E labeled 'NEURONS', containing multiple neuronal cell types (ADF, AWB, URX, RIC) and their associated genes.

compartments.label: ADF
compartments.snippet: Blue pentagon labeled 'ADF' within the NEURONS compartment in panel E; associated with tph-1 and serotonin (5-HT) production.

compartments.label: AWB
compartments.snippet: Green triangle labeled 'AWB' within the NEURONS compartment in panel E; associated with ser-6.

compartments.label: URX
compartments.snippet: Yellow hexagon labeled 'URX' within the NEURONS compartment in panel E; associated with mod-1.

compartments.label: RIC
compartments.snippet: Red hexagon labeled 'RIC' within the NEURONS compartment in panel E; associated with tbh-1 and octopamine (Oct).

compartments.label: INTESTINE
compartments.snippet: Green rounded rectangle on the right side of panel E labeled 'INTESTINE', containing nhr-76, ATGL, and downstream beta-oxidation / fat loss.

genes.snippet: Labeled next to the ADF neuron in panel E; also compared as a mutant (tph-1) in panels A and B.
Labeled inside the URX hexagon in panel E; also referenced in transgene Pflp-8::mod-1SAS in panels C and D.
Labeled next to the AWB triangle in panel E within the NEURONS compartment.
Labeled next to the RIC hexagon in panel E within the NEURONS compartment; associated with octopamine (Oct) production.
Shown in the INTESTINE compartment in panel E; depicted as activating ATGL with an arrow.
Shown in the INTESTINE compartment in panel E downstream of nhr-76; leads to beta-oxidation and fat loss. Also referenced by transgene Patgl-1::GFP used as reporter in panels A-D.

tentative_edges.snippet: In panel E, tph-1 in ADF produces 5-HT (serotonin), which signals to mod-1 in URX via an arrow labeled '5-HT'.
In panel E, tbh-1 in RIC produces octopamine (Oct) with a dashed arrow pointing back toward AWB (ser-6), suggesting a feedback or lateral signal.
In panel E, a dashed arrow labeled 'endocrine signal' connects the NEURONS compartment (emanating from URX/mod-1) to the INTESTINE compartment.
In panel E within the INTESTINE, an arrow from nhr-76 points to ATGL, indicating nhr-76-mediated transcriptional activation.
In panel E within the INTESTINE, an arrow from ATGL leads to the label 'β-oxidation / Fat loss', indicating ATGL drives these downstream outcomes.

tentative_edges.relation: negatively regulates atgl-1 expression (loss of serotonin reduces GFP reporter)
tentative_edges.snippet: Panels A and B show that tph-1 mutants have significantly reduced Patgl-1::GFP fluorescence compared to wild-type, indicating tph-1 (serotonin) is required for full atgl-1 expression.

tentative_edges.relation: blocking mod-1 in URX suppresses 5-HT-induced atgl-1 upregulation
tentative_edges.snippet: Panels C and D show that expression of mod-1SAS (dominant negative) in URX neurons (Pflp-8::mod-1SAS) blocks the 5-HT-induced increase in Patgl-1::GFP fluorescence in the intestine.
